rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as eq helpers.
Validate that AGREE equation and constant declarations use only valid scalar types (int, real, bool) and match the expected types of expressions.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are either component ports, declared eq variables, or valid constants within the same annex scope.
AGREE contracts must not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types that are not present in the AADL model's configured dependencies.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and must be placed in appropriate component type or implementation scopes.
Assignments in AGREE must reference valid output ports or eq variables on the left-hand side.
anti_patterns: Avoid referencing identifiers in AGREE that are not declared or visible in the current component's scope, as this leads to 'Couldn't resolve reference' errors.
Do not use invalid scalar type names in AGREE const or eq declarations; use int, real, or bool instead.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations not pointing to anything
Component type and implementation structural extraction
